title: The Youth Welfare Fund Ordinance, 1985
description: An Ordinance to provide for the establishment of the Youth Welfare Fund. WHEREAS it is expedient to provide for the establishment of the Youth Welfare Fund and for matters ancillary thereto; NOW, THEREFORE, in pursuance of the Proclamation of the 24th March, 1982, and in exercise of all powers enabling him in that behalf, the President is pleased to make and promulgate the following Ordinance:-

provisions.title: 1. Short title
provisions.content: 1. This Ordinance may be called the Youth Welfare Fund Ordinance, 1985 .

provisions.title: 2. Definitions
3. Establishment of the Fund
4. Utilisation of the Fund
provisions.content:
4. (1) The moneys of the Fund shall be utilised primarily for the purposes of-
(a) making of grants-in-aid to youth organisations;
[(b) awarding prizes and making grants to youths.]
(2) All expenses in connection with the administration of the Fund shall be met out of the moneys of the Fund.

provisions.title: 5. Board
6. Functions of the Board
7. Meetings of the Board
provisions.content:
7. (1) Save as hereinafter provided, the Board shall follow such procedure as may be prescribed.
(2) The meeting of the Board shall be held on such date and at such time and place as may be determined by the Chairman:
Provided that at least four meetings shall be held in each year.
(3) To constitute a quorum at a meeting of the Board, not less than one-third of the total number of its members shall be present.
(4) All meetings of the Board shall be presided over by the Chairman and, in his absence, by a member authorised by the Chairman in this behalf.

provisions.title: 8. Selection Committee
9. Condition for making grants-in-aid, etc.
10. Appointment of officers, etc
provisions.content: 10. The Secretary of the Board may, with the approval of the Board, appoint such officers and other employees as he may consider necessary to assist him in the discharge of his functions.

provisions.title: 11. Audit and accounts
provisions.content:
11. (1) The accounts of the Fund shall be maintained in such manner and form as the Comptroller and Auditor-General of Bangladesh, hereinafter referred to as the Auditor-General, may, from time to time, direct.
(2) The accounts of the Fund shall be audited by the Auditor-General.
(3) A statement of audited accounts shall be furnished to the Government by the Board.

provisions.title: 12. Power to make rules
provisions.content: 12. The Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, make rules for the purpose of giving effect to all or any of the provisions of this Ordinance.

definitions.definition: the Youth Welfare Fund Management Board established under section 5
the Youth Welfare Fund established under section 3
the Selection Committee constituted under section 8
the Bangladesh Banks (Nationalisation) Order, 1972 (P.O. No. 26 of 1972)
prescribed by rules made under this Ordinance
any organisation which is formed for the purpose of development of youth and [youth] welfare and registered under any law for the time being in force.
